source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two uniform discs in a vertical plane of masses $M_{1}$ and $M_{2}$ with radii $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ respectively have a thread wound about their circumferences, and are thus connected as shown in Fig. 1.158. The first disc has fixed frictionless horizontal axis of rotation through its center. Set up the equations to determine the acceleration of the center of mass of the second disc if it falls freely.
extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL object representation rather than a literal file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL object representation instead of a literal file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式写有“as shown in Fig. 1.158”，解题对象的连接方式依赖示意图。
输入字段 `requires_image=true` 且 `text_dominant=false`，说明图像不是可有可无的辅助信息。
系统构型涉及两圆盘、绕线连接、轴固定与下落方向，空间/结构关系需要图像确认。
multi_step: 答案不是单一数值，而是需要建立一组动力学方程，包含平动、两个转动方程和一个约束方程。
需要组合多个物理关系：受力分析、转动动力学、几何/无滑动约束。
题目要求“Set up the equations to determine...”，表明至少需要两步以上的结构化推导。
verifiability: 给定答案形式为明确的方程组，包含 `M2 ddot{x}=M2 g-F`、两个转动方程和约束关系，可逐条核验。
输入中 `answer_present=true` 且答案文本列出了具体符号关系与转动惯量表达式。
虽然符号命名和方程排列可能有等价变体，但核心约束与动力学关系可被规则检查。

equation-set answers may have notation/order variants even when mathematically equivalent

图像依赖度高，但当前已有可用图像文件，能够支撑题目中的结构关系与受力/转动建模。
初步评分整体较高，尤其是图像依赖性和可验证性较强，适合作为候选样本保留。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two uniform discs in a vertical plane of masses $M_{1}$ and $M_{2}$ with radii $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ respectively have a thread wound about their circumferences, and are thus connected as shown in Fig. 1.158. The first disc has fixed frictionless horizontal axis of rotation through its center. Set up the equations to determine the acceleration of the center of mass of the second disc if it falls freely.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL object representation rather than a literal file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Let $F$ be the tension in the thread, $x_{1}$ the distance of the center of mass of disc 2 from that of disc 1, and $\dot{\theta}_{1},\dot{\theta}_{2}$ the angular velocities of the discs, as shown in Fig. 1.158. We have the equations of motion $$ M_{2}\ \ddot{x}=M_{2}g-F,\quad I_{1}\ \ddot{\theta}_{1}=FR_{1},\quad I_{2}\ \ddot{\theta}_{2}=FR_{2}, $$ where $I_{1}=\frac{m_{1}R_{1}^{2}}{2},\; I_{2}=\frac{m_{2}R_{2}^{2}}{2}$. Disc 2 also has the constraint $$ \dot{x}=R_{1}\ \dot{\theta}_{1}+R_{2}\ \dot{\theta}_{2} $$ or $$ \ddot{x}=R_{1}\ \ddot{\theta}_{1}+R_{2}\ \ddot{\theta}_{2}. $$ From the four equations the unknowns $\ddot{\theta}_{1},\ddot{\theta}_{2},\ddot{x}$ and $F$ can be determined.
